title: Impact of Progressive Disclosure Agent Skills on Context Efficiency
subtitle: Example Author Team · Example Data (corresponding to papers/sample_paper.md)

slides.title: Table of Contents
bullets: Research Background and Problem
Method Overview: Three-Level Progressive Disclosure
Key Results: Context and Cache
Limitations and Discussion
Summary

slides.title: Research Background and Problem
bullets: The more tasks an Agent supports, the more linearly a single system prompt expands
Long prompts incur triple costs: token cost, attention dilution, and cache prefix invalidation
Core contradiction: letting the Agent "know its own capabilities" without long-term context occupation

slides.title: Method Overview (Overall Approach)
bullets: First give the Agent a thin directory, then load the full Skill when needed
Layer 1: At startup, inject only each Skill's name + description (hundreds of tokens)
Layer 2: When a task triggers, load the complete SKILL.md as a tool result

slides.title: Method Overview (Key Mechanism)
bullets: Layer 3: On-demand reading of reference.md, script source code, and other sub-documents
description should be written as "routing conditions" rather than "feature introduction"
Bundle executable scripts to upgrade knowledge into actionable capabilities

slides.title: Key Results (Efficiency Metrics)
bullets: Resident context drops from thousands of tokens to directory-level hundreds of tokens
Constant number of tools, stable prefix, significantly improved KV Cache hit rate

slides.title: Key Results (Effect Comparison)
bullets: On tasks requiring specialized Skills, success rate is on par with the "full injection" baseline
Adding counterexamples (Don't use when) significantly improves routing accuracy
Reduces false triggers on irrelevant tasks

slides.title: Limitations and Discussion
bullets: Triggering relies on the model's "metacognition"; misjudgment may cause missed Skill loading
Third-party Skills introduce a new prompt injection surface; their content must be reviewed before loading

slides.title: Summary
bullets: Progressive disclosure transforms "stuffing everything at once" into "on-demand loading"
Almost no loss in task success rate, while greatly reducing resident context
It is a practical paradigm for building scalable agent capability systems.
